_meta.description: Authoritative reference list of CSO/EDM monitors tracked by the FRBC tides dashboard. Bundled into the app at deploy time (not fetched live) -- update this file and redeploy to apply changes. app.py should contain no hardcoded permit IDs; it reads zones and monitors from here. tunnel_connected, geographic zone, and Richmond/Putney placement are tracked as fully independent attributes -- do not infer one from another.
_meta.review_instructions: 1) Re-fetch tw_specific_sites in full -- use web_fetch with text_content_token_limit >= 150000, it truncates mid-alphabet otherwise and silently returns A-R only. 2) Re-run scripts/verify_cso_monitors.py against tw_opendata_api_status to catch newly retired/renamed permits. 3) Check tw_edm_annual_return for a newer edition (published ~March/April each year). 4) For EVERY monitor, tunnel_connected must be one of true/false/not_applicable, each backed by a direct quote-paraphrase from a named source in tunnel_status_source -- never infer from watercourse, geography, or clustering with other monitors. If a monitor's tunnel status cannot be directly evidenced, tunnel_connected must be null and tunnel_status_source must explain what is missing, not contain a guess.

tunnel_connected_values.true: Source explicitly states present-tense connection to the London Tideway Tunnels system
tunnel_connected_values.false: Source explicitly states the CSO is NOT directly connected (tw_specific_sites uses standard wording: 'not directly connected to the Thames Tideway Tunnel route. However the tunnel will influence this CSO...')
tunnel_connected_values.not_applicable: Site has no CSO/tunnel-status paragraph on tw_specific_sites at all -- it's an STW, pumping/transfer station, or generic catchment entry, not a CSO subject to a tunnel-connection determination
tunnel_connected_values.null: Not yet checked against any source -- requires follow-up, do not treat as false

tw_api_docs.title: Thames Water Open Data API documentation portal
tw_api_docs.what_it_provides: Endpoint schemas: query parameters (offset, limit, locationName, permitNumber, alertType, dateStart/dateEnd etc.), example request/response bodies, field names (lowercase camelCase: permitNumber, locationName, alertType, datetime).
tw_api_docs.caveat: Likely a Salesforce Experience Cloud portal (URL pattern matches data.thameswater.co.uk/s/login) -- not search-indexed, must be fetched/read directly by a human or pasted in.

tw_opendata_api_status.title: Thames Water Open Data API v2 -- discharge/status endpoint
tw_opendata_api_status.what_it_provides: Live ground truth: which permits currently exist and are reporting, with locationName, x/y coordinates, alertStatus. No API key required.
tw_opendata_api_status.caveat: Paginate with offset/limit; observed working limit is 200/page (larger values returned HTTP 500). See scripts/verify_cso_monitors.py.

tw_opendata_api_alerts.title: Thames Water Open Data API v2 -- discharge/alerts endpoint
tw_opendata_api_alerts.what_it_provides: Historic discharge Start/Stop events per permit, used by app.py to compute discharge hours per monitor over the 48h window.

tw_edm_annual_return.title: EDM Storm Overflow Regulatory Annual Return (Thames Water)
tw_edm_annual_return.url: REPLACE_WITH_SOURCE_URL_WHEN_KNOWN -- file was supplied directly by Andrew as edm-annual-return-2025.xlsx
tw_edm_annual_return.what_it_provides: Official mapping of Unique ID (legacy TWL00xxx scheme) to EA Permit Reference (current scheme used by the live API), plus official site name, receiving watercourse, NGR grid reference, and storm discharge asset type for every monitor nationally.
tw_edm_annual_return.caveat: Published annually, this copy dated 2025 (covers reporting year 2024/25). A newer edition will exist by the next review date.

tw_specific_sites.title: Thames Water -- Investment plans for storm discharge sites
tw_specific_sites.what_it_provides: Authoritative site-by-site statement of Tideway Tunnel connection status, organised alphabetically by site NAME (not permit number). Present-tense 'is now connected to the London Tideway Tunnels system' = connected. 'This CSO is not directly connected to the Thames Tideway Tunnel route. However the tunnel will influence this CSO...' = not connected. Sites with only an investment-timeline paragraph and no tunnel-status paragraph at all = not_applicable (not a CSO in scope of the tunnel determination).
tw_specific_sites.caveat: Very large page (550+ entries A-W). MUST fetch with text_content_token_limit >= 150000 or it truncates silently around the letter R with no error. Page states 'last updated April 2026' -- re-check this date on next review.

tw_investment_plan.title: Thames Water -- Investing in river health (storm overflow reduction plan)
tw_investment_plan.what_it_provides: Wider context: company-level storm overflow reduction targets and timelines, linked from every individual site entry in tw_specific_sites.
tw_investment_plan.caveat: Not yet read in full -- worth checking if litigation needs broader regulatory-commitment context.

tideway_tunnel_order_2014.title: The Thames Water Utilities Limited (Thames Tideway Tunnel) Order 2014, Schedule 1
tideway_tunnel_order_2014.what_it_provides: Original statutory development consent naming specific CSOs and their connection works (e.g. confirms Putney Bridge CSO and West Putney Storm Relief CSO connection tunnels by Work Number). Useful corroboration where tw_specific_sites wording is ambiguous.
tideway_tunnel_order_2014.caveat: Describes the planned 2014 scheme, not confirmed current operational status -- always cross-check against tw_specific_sites for current status.

tideway_cso_navigation_risk.title: Tideway -- CSO Discharges, Risk to navigation
tideway_cso_navigation_risk.what_it_provides: Lists the seven CSO locations whose physical outfall position was moved further into the river by the Tideway project, for mariner passage-planning purposes: Putney Embankment (PUTEF), Chelsea Embankment (CHEEF), Heathwall Pumping Station (HEAPS) Nine Elms, Albert Bridge (ALBEF), Victoria Embankment (VCTEF), Blackfriars Bridge (BLABF), King Edward Memorial Park (KEMPF). All seven are tunnel-connected CSOs by definition (the relocation/DRA process only applies to connected sites). Corroborates Putney Bridge CSO and Victoria CSO already in our tunnel-connected set, and identifies Heathwall (Nine Elms) and Chelsea Embankment/Ranelagh as additional confirmed tunnel-connected sites not yet in our tracked monitor list.
tideway_cso_navigation_risk.caveat: Covers only 7 of the ~34 tunnel-connected CSOs nationally -- specifically those whose physical discharge point moved during construction. Absence from this list does NOT mean a CSO is not tunnel-connected; consult tw_specific_sites for the full determination.

Hammersmith.description: Within approx 500m of FRBC pontoon at Hammersmith Bridge. Independent of tunnel_connected status.

Richmond to Putney.description: Manually placed -- upstream and downstream of Hammersmith within the Richmond-to-Putney stretch. River geography is not linear so placement is judged per-site, not a fixed km boundary. Independent of tunnel_connected status -- a monitor here may or may not be tunnel-connected.

Beyond.description: Manually placed -- upstream beyond Richmond, or downstream beyond Putney into central London. Independent of tunnel_connected status.

Tideway Tunnel.description: Display zone for monitors confirmed tunnel_connected: true per tw_specific_sites. This is a presentation grouping, not a different attribute -- every monitor here also has a true geographic position; check the monitors{} entry for its actual location. Includes two sites (Heathwall, Ranelagh) added 2026-06-30 with permit numbers not yet confirmed -- see needs_followup.

dropped_monitors._note: Permits previously tracked that were removed during the 2026-06 zone rebuild, with reasons
dropped_monitors.TWL00371: Not found in EDM annual return or live API -- likely a typo or defunct ID from original (undocumented) zone list construction. No replacement identified.

_config.description: Runtime config consumed by app.py. Edit this file and redeploy to change monitors, zones, or discharge time windows without touching app.py or index.html.
